description: errorBoundary §19.6.5 priority #2 — a caught error variant WITHOUT a `renders` clause falls to the boundary's `fallback`. loadItem('slow') fails LoadError.Timeout (no renders) at mount; the boundary's `fallback` (eb-fallback) is shown, and the sibling variant's markup (eb-notfound) does NOT appear (that variant wasn't the one thrown). Complements error-boundary/variant-renders (priority #1). Sibling content outside the boundary survives (§19.6.4). OQ1: whole-tree dom OMITTED (impl#1 wraps the boundary subtree in a private container div); the impl-neutral contract is the anchored author-class assertions.
rationale: §19.6.3 priority #2: if the caught error variant has NO `renders` clause and the boundary has a `fallback`, the `fallback` markup is displayed. Timeout is a nullary no-renders variant -> eb-fallback shows. NotFound's renders never fires (not the thrown variant). §19.6.6: the `!`-call inside the boundary needs no explicit handling -> the whole E-ERROR-* family stays silent.
